sub_pixel_system.notes: No sub-pixel system documentation available in sources

special_mechanics.name: Evolution System
special_mechanics.description: Player character body is divided into eight sections that can be upgraded using evolution points. Upgrades change appearance and grant new abilities including tail swings, greater jumping ability, and increased movement speed.

special_mechanics.name: Attack Mechanics
special_mechanics.description: Player can defeat enemies by biting, ramming with horns, jumping on them, or jumping over them to avoid combat.

special_mechanics.name: Health Restoration
special_mechanics.description: Player can restore health by consuming meat from defeated foes, eating nearby plant life, or undergoing selective evolution.

special_mechanics.name: Death Mechanic
special_mechanics.description: No game over; if player loses all health, character is revived by Gaia and sent back to map screen with roughly half evolution points deducted.

tier_notes: Tier 2 - Empirical/Qualitative data. No precise physics parameters documented in available sources. Game mechanics are described narratively (jumping ability improvements, movement speed increases through evolution) without quantitative values. Movement physics are dependent on player's current evolutionary form.
sources_used: Wikipedia - E.V.O.: Search for Eden (Gameplay and Mechanics sections)
